servinfo.title: Curtain Air Bag (Inflator) Module Removal and Installation

attention1.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions when performing service on the curtain air bag (inflator) module can result in personal injury or non-activation of the air bag in the event of a collision.

item.ptxt: Never attempt to disassemble or repair an air bag (inflator) module. If any abnormality is found, replace the module with a new one.

Carefully read Precautions for Air Bag System Service , Precautions for Handling and Storage of Air Bag System Components and Precautions for Disposal of Air Bag and Seat Belt Pretensioner and follow all of the instructions provided.
Do not install curtain air bag (inflator) module while twisted or bent. Otherwise, curtain air bag (inflator) module may not deploy and injury may result.

attention4.ptxt: An air bag (inflator) module that has been dropped may not operate correctly.
Never use an air bag (inflator) module that has been dropped. Replace the module with a new one.

attention3.ptxt: Refer to Repair and Inspection Required after Accident before replacing a deployed air bag.

s1.ptxt: Remove front pillar trim and quarter inner upper trim.
Disconnect curtain air bag (inflator) module connector (3) as shown in figure.
Remove curtain air bag (inflator) module bolts (4) and remove curtain air bag (inflator) module (1).

servinfosub.title: Installation
servinfosub.ptxt: Reverse removal procedure noting the following points.

item.ptxt: Install curtain air bag (inflator) module without twisting marking line (1) on strap part.
Install claws (2) of curtain air bag (inflator) module to vehicle holes (3).
Use new precoated curtain air bag (inflator) module bolts and tighten them to specified torque.

attention3.ptxt: Check that bracket of clamping part to body is securely fixed after tightening curtain air bag (inflator) module bolts.
